{{Class Custom Image|color-h=222|color-s=21%|color-l=51%|hexagon=3|safety=Unsafe|security=Psychologically manipulateing|entity=High Entity Count |class=3|bg-image=Mirror.jpg|text-color=White|header=Threat Index: Class}} '''Level 305''' is the 306th level of the backrooms.
Description
{{Blurimage|file=Mirror.jpg|caption=An image of '''Level 305'''. Photographer now residing in [[Level 87]].|hovertext=No reflections... so who's taking the photo?}} '''Level 305''', colloquially referred to as "Reflections," is an infinite labyrinthine complex comprising crystal clear mirrors, concrete floors, and dimly lit lights. Additionally, the fluorescent lights are known to produce a quiet hum-buzz that increases and decreases its volume at random. The level tends to have a frigid temperature of around 0°C; however, the temperature may rarely drop down to -5°C, which causes all mirrors to form thin ice sheets. Recently, [[M.E.G.]] researchers discovered that '''Level 305''' has phenomenal "glitches" that affect mirrors throughout the maze. When the mirrors appear "glitchy," it is recommended to avoid touching them, as reports have stated that wanderers who touched them become physically inverted in a slow yet painful process. Mirrors within the level are occasionally cracked or completely shattered. As a result, travelers should avoid them at all costs due to the ruptured mirrors serving as an entrance to [[The Void]]. Throughout '''Level 305''', electronics such as radios, telephones, and disassembled Wi-Fi routers are scattered across the concrete floor. Oddly enough, despite their wear and tear and lack of wires, they are still somewhat functional. Parallel with [[Level 3]] and many other levels, the somewhat functional Wi-Fi routers are an excellent source of cellular signal with the password being identical between all of them. Occasionally, random blackouts can occur, casting sections of the level into complete darkness. Due the periodic darkness, some mirrors can conduct a dim glow; it is heavily unadvised to gaze at mirrors for too long, as visual illusions may occur, causing wanderers to see deformed, unknown, or monstrous faces. While individuals experiencing these hallucinations may perceive them as real, these occurrences are actually a result of the brain's deceptive mechanisms. There is no physical issue with their faces, and no undiscovered beings are inhabiting the surroundings to date. Lastly, one other section of the mirror labyrinth has been thoroughly explored and documented. The corresponding area is The Neon Mirrors; they are also noteworthy for their unique anomalies and hidden properties. ===Neon Mirrors=== {{Blurimage|file=Neon Mirrors.JPG|caption=A photograph taken of '''Level 305's''' deeper region.}} Neon Mirrors typically occur deep into the level when a wanderer has been traveling for quite some time and distance. Undeterred by the rest of the level, Neon mirrors are composed of blue neon light strips surrounding them, basking them in a soft, cobalt light. These mirrors also seem immune to blackouts, making these parts of the level slightly safer, although the number of entities here is shockingly higher. Neon Mirrors have another distinct difference in contrast to ordinary mirrors, with that being the wanderer's reflection. Instead of their reflection mimicking their movements, they will seem laggy, as if not following the wanderer's movements immediately. One wanderer has even reported that in several of the Neon Mirrors, his reflections stared at him, not bothering to attempt to mimic his movements. Consequently, this may suggest that Neon Mirrors are their own entity, away from ordinary mirrors.
Addendum
===The Glitch=== '''The Glitch''' is an anomalous event that occurs in levels and sub-levels linked to [[The Broken]]. Although, it is noted that sub-levels tend to experience the event more frequently. In '''Level 305''', '''The Glitch''' begins when all of its mirrors start glitching out - it will worsen until the mirrors cannot handle it anymore, causing the corruption to burst and spread outwards. Once '''Level 305''' becomes fully affected by the '''The Glitch''', the entire labyrinth succumbs to greater corruption, temporarily blinding one's peripheral view. In addition to that, all technology will begin to malfunction: contacting any group is pointless. After 30 minutes pass, the glitching will come to an utter stop; thus, causing the communication systems to have a signal once more. Oddly enough, wanderers with epilepsy whom witnessed '''The Glitch''' firsthand never experience seizures. Journal entries regarding '''Level 305''' was found on [[Level 64]].
''Day 26''
'''Mirror, Mirror on the Wall'''

After I thought all of my hope was lost, after nearly dying in [[Level 519|The Hall of Dull Flames]], I slipped and fell into an opening of the baby-blue stained floor. I sighed as I thought I'd entered [[Level 519|The Abyss]], but shockingly, I entered a mysterious mirror maze. I wondered if this was another section of [[Level 519]], although now I realize this maze is a new level. As I progressed forward, I saw a [[Entity 8|dog-like creature]] stare at me; this made me periodically paralyzed out of spite of fear. As soon as the Hound charged at me, I ran in the opposite direction, not even looking back. It has been an hour, and I think I have lost that grotesque entity. I've seen cellular devices lying awkwardly on the ground that work surprisingly well despite heavy damage.
''Day 27''
'''Neon Anomaly'''

How long have I traversed the level? I have no idea. I managed to survive even with the lack of food and water; I think I am going insane. Despite the struggles, I moved forward until I saw the mirrors become neon blue. Anomalously, some of my reflections didn't even follow my movements and just stood there. I curiously wondered if those types of mirrors were entities of some sort. Shortly after my inquisitiveness, disaster struck; the level's light and technological sources suddenly stopped working, and entities flooded the hellhole. With the last of my stability, I ran until I saw something, a [[Level 410|blood-red door]].
Sentient Prescence
The only entities that have been documented to inhabit '''Level 305''' are [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], [[Entity 64|Cells]], and in blackouts, [[Entity 3|Smilers]].
Colonies and Outposts
Creating any settlement is simply not feasible due to the entities.
Entrances and Exits
{{Blurimage|file=Mirror Rooms 2.0.jpg|caption=An area of '''Level 305''' transitioning into the '''Neon Mirrors'''.}} ===Entrances=== *Doors in [[Level 483]] may lead one here. *Finding a mirror in [[Level 3]] and entering it will lead one here. *Going through a broken glass door at the end of a hallway on [[Level 130]] will lead one here. *One may end up here randomly from [[Level 17]]. *No-clipping through a mirror in [[Level 366]] will send one here. *Noclipping on a station at [[Level 870]] will send one here. ===Exits=== *Entering a blood covered door will take one to [[Level 410]]. *Following a blue glint will eventually lead one to [[Level 306]]. *One may end up on [[Level 17]] by walking for an undetermined period of time. *Finding a door with bars and entering it will lead one to [[Level 3]]. *No-clipping through a mirror that has a white neon light above it will take one to [[Level 256]]. *No-clipping through a neon mirror has a slim chance of taking you to [[Level 699]]. ---- {{Redirect|Prev=304|Next=306}} {{LinkCollapse|num=1|initial='''Open Credits'''|final='''Close Credits'''|body=
